_comment: What the upstream LLM provider actually received. The user's IP and hostnames are gone; only PromptGuard's per-conversation random tokens reached the wire. PromptGuard's reverse path then substituted the originals back into the streamed response so the user sees their real values. Per-token random suffix is from secrets.token_hex(8) (DEC-012); 64 bits of unguessable entropy per token.
user_text_sent_to_upstream_excerpt: ...Echo this back exactly: my server is at [INTERNAL_IP_02ef306c7b29d172]
user_visible_response: Confirmed token echo: claude.ai, settings.local, github.com, 10.0.13.42.
note_user_email_was_masked: An email address (synthetic stand-in here: alice@internal-corp.example) appeared in claude CLI's system-reminder block. Default policy MASKs emails (no token-map ledger) so the upstream saw `[EMAIL_REDACTED]` and the user-visible response did not refer to the email at all (MASK is one-way by design: see threat-model A6). The original captured artifact contained the owner's real work address; it has been replaced here with the synthetic form before publication.
